profile.name: Ryan Graves
roles: U.S. Navy F/A-18 Fighter Pilot, VFA-11 Red Rippers
Founder and Executive Director, Americans for Safe Aerospace (ASA)
profile.summary: Retired U.S. Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet pilot who documented sustained UAP encounters experienced by his squadron off the East Coast of the United States between 2014 and 2015 following the Navy's upgrade to the AN/APG-79 AESA radar system. Unlike the single-event Nimitz encounter, Graves and his VFA-11 squadron reported near-daily UAP sightings over a period of months - the most sustained documented military UAP encounter series in the modern era. Testified before the House Oversight Committee on July 26, 2023, representing active-duty and recently separated pilots reluctant to come forward due to professional stigma. Founded Americans for Safe Aerospace in 2022 to provide a structured, professionally credible reporting channel for military and commercial aviators.

key_events.event: Commissioned as a Naval Aviator; begins active duty assignment flying F/A-18 Super Hornets

VFA-11 Red Rippers squadron upgrades to the AN/APG-79 AESA radar system, dramatically improving detection resolution. Within months, pilots begin tracking unidentified objects at high altitude over the U.S. East Coast operating area

Over a period of months extending into 2015, Graves and fellow VFA-11 pilots report near-daily encounters with UAP in the Navy's Virginia Beach-to-North Carolina operating area. Objects tracked at 30,000 ft, holding station in Category 4 hurricane-force winds, with no visible means of propulsion. One near-collision reported.

The New York Times publishes 'Navy pilots report encountering UFOs.' Graves is quoted as a named source - a rare step for active-duty military - describing the persistent nature of the encounters and the professional risk of reporting them

Founds Americans for Safe Aerospace (ASA), a nonprofit designed specifically to serve as a structured reporting and advocacy channel for military and commercial pilots experiencing UAP encounters, eliminating the career stigma that suppresses reporting

Testifies under oath before the House Oversight Committee subcommittee on UAP alongside David Grusch and David Fravor. Presents the VFA-11 encounters as evidence of a systemic reporting failure and a genuine aviation safety crisis. States he is aware of military pilots who continue to observe UAP regularly
Testifies before the Senate Armed Services Committee on UAP. ASA formally presents data showing significant underreporting of aviator UAP encounters due to professional stigma

ASA publishes pilot survey data showing that a large proportion of commercial and military aviators have encountered unexplained phenomena but do not report due to career concerns. Continues congressional engagement alongside disclosure advocates

east_coast_encounters.location: U.S. Navy East Coast operating area, Virginia Beach to North Carolina
east_coast_encounters.aircraft: F/A-18 Super Hornet, AN/APG-79 AESA radar
east_coast_encounters.altitude: Typically 30,000 ft; objects holding station at altitude in extreme wind conditions
east_coast_encounters.behavior: Stationary in Category 4 hurricane-force winds; no visible propulsion; rapid repositioning
east_coast_encounters.near_miss: At least one near-collision reported by VFA-11 pilots
east_coast_encounters.significance: Unlike single-event encounters (Nimitz, Roosevelt), the VFA-11 encounters represent the most sustained documented military UAP encounter series, suggesting persistent presence in designated training airspace rather than isolated events

claims.reporting_environment: Graves has testified that the professional culture strongly discourages UAP reporting. Pilots who file reports risk being referred to flight surgeons and grounded for psychological evaluation. The result is systematic underreporting across both military and commercial aviation.

associated_people.name: David Fravor
associated_people.relationship: Testified alongside Graves at the July 26, 2023 House Oversight hearing. Both represent the military aviator class of UAP witnesses - those whose training and equipment give their observations maximum credibility.

associated_people.name: David Grusch
associated_people.relationship: Co-testified with Graves at the July 26, 2023 hearing. While Grusch's testimony addressed crash retrieval programs, Graves grounded the hearing in documented, observable encounters - the two testimonies complemented each other at the congressional level.

associated_people.name: Karl Nell
associated_people.relationship: Graves is affiliated with the Sol Foundation alongside Nell, Grusch, and Nolan as part of the ecosystem attempting to institutionalize UAP research.

disclosures.title: Navy Pilots Report Encountering UFOs on an Almost Daily Basis in 2014 and 2015
disclosures.interviewer: Helene Cooper, Ralph Blumenthal, Leslie Kean
disclosures.notes: First named public disclosure. Graves is quoted describing the nature, frequency, and duration of encounters. Established the East Coast UAP encounter series as a documented record distinct from the Nimitz incident.

disclosures.title: House Oversight Subcommittee on UAP - Testimony
disclosures.notes: Under-oath testimony before the full subcommittee. Described the VFA-11 encounters in detail and argued that the absence of reporting infrastructure is itself evidence of institutional failure. One of three witnesses at the most significant congressional UAP hearing in modern history.

disclosures.title: Senate Armed Services Committee - UAP Testimony
disclosures.notes: Follow-up Senate testimony presenting Americans for Safe Aerospace survey data on the scope of unreported aviator UAP encounters.

sources.title: New York Times - 'Navy Pilots Report Encountering UFOs on an Almost Daily Basis' (2019)
sources.notes: First public naming of Graves; foundational source for VFA-11 East Coast encounter documentation

sources.title: House Oversight Subcommittee UAP Hearing - C-SPAN (2023)
sources.notes: Full transcript and video of Graves's July 26, 2023 congressional testimony

sources.title: Americans for Safe Aerospace
sources.notes: Graves's nonprofit; publishes pilot survey data and policy advocacy materials

sources.title: Wikipedia - Ryan Graves (aviator)
sources.notes: Biographical reference and documented encounter timeline

supporting: Active-duty Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet pilot with documented operational flight hours in the VFA-11 Red Rippers squadron - his aviation expertise is directly applicable to assessing the anomalous behavior he observed
Encounters were corroborated by multiple VFA-11 squadron members over a period of months, providing independent witness depth well beyond a single-observer account
Coming forward publicly as a named source in the June 2019 New York Times article while still affiliated with the Navy represented a significant professional and reputational cost
Testified under oath before the House Oversight Committee on July 26, 2023, making him legally accountable for the accuracy of his statements

Founded Americans for Safe Aerospace in 2022, a structured nonprofit advocacy organization focused on pilot safety reporting - demonstrating a sustained, principled commitment to the issue rather than episodic media engagement

His reported object characteristics - stationary against high-velocity winds, no visible propulsion system, sudden repositioning - are described consistently across multiple accounts and witnesses from the same encounter series
contradicting: The objects encountered by VFA-11 have not been officially identified or confirmed as non-human in origin by the U.S. Navy, Air Force, or any other government body
His sustained public role through Americans for Safe Aerospace creates a reputational investment in UAP being treated as a serious, unresolved phenomenon

The sensor and radar data from the 2014-2015 East Coast encounters has not been declassified or made available for independent technical analysis, limiting external verification of the performance characteristics he describes

Graves himself has consistently acknowledged that he cannot determine the origin, nature, or intent of the objects his squadron observed - his testimony establishes unexplained presence, not its source

Some analysts have noted that the AN/APG-79 AESA radar system installed on VFA-11 aircraft around 2014 was new to the squadron at the time of the encounters, raising questions about whether crews were fully calibrated to its detection artifacts in novel operating conditions

career_connections.relationship: Elizondo identified military pilot stigma through AATIP; Graves June 2019 NY Times account directly validated this finding
Mellon provided behind-the-scenes Congressional coordination and briefings supporting Americans for Safe Aerospace
Co-testified at July 26, 2023 House Oversight hearing; Graves encounter evidence grounded the program-level disclosures
Co-testified with Fravor at July 2023 hearing; both represent the military aviator class of UAP witnesses
Nell testified alongside Graves and is affiliated with Sol Foundation, which overlaps with ASA advocacy work
